name: Olive Potato Frittata
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons olive oil, or cooking oil
2 medium potatoes (11oz each), thinly sliced, about 2 cups
1 medium onion, cut into thin wedges
2 tablespoons snipped fresh oregano -OR- 1 tsp dried
1/4 cup finely shredded provolone or Parmesan
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch broiler-proof or regular skillet, heat oil.
2. Add potatoes, onion, garlic, ¼ teaspoon of the salt, and the pepper.
3. Cover and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes.
4. Turn the potato mixture with a spatula.
5. Cover and cook for 5-6 more minutes until potatoes are tender, turning the mixture once more.
6. In a medium mixing bowl, beat together eggs, oregano, and the remaining salt.
7. Pour egg mixture over hot potato mixture.
8. Sprinkle with olives.
9. Cook over medium heat.
10. As the mixture sets, run a spatula around the edge of the skillet, lifting the egg mixture to allow the uncooked portion to flow underneath.
11. Continue cooking and lifting edges until the egg mixture is almost set (the surface will be moist).
12. Place the broiler-proof skillet under the broiler 4-5 inches from the heat.
13. Broil for 1-2 minutes or till top is set, or if using a regular skillet, remove the skillet from the heat; cover and let stand for 3-4 minutes or till the top is set.
14. Sprinkle the top with finely shredded provolone or Parmesan cheese.
15. Serve immediately.
Makes 4 main-dish servings.
Per serving: 340 Cal / 2g Fat / 7g Sat Fat / 431mg Chol / 671mg Sodium / 21g Carb / 2g Fiber / 17g Prot.
